prompt.content:
Use file “Onc_Patient 7” to access the entire medical history / record for the patient. Please use the patient tab as the source of truth for all demographic information. Disregard patient identifiers (Name, Age, DOB, MRN) found within the text of the clinicalnotes tab as they have been shifted for de-identification purposes

---

Onc Patient 7 comes to the oncology clinic for a follow-up visit. Based on the patient's chart, conversation with the doctor, and investigations, what is the most appropriate next treatment option? What is the dosing and frequency? Explain your reasoning.

extra.task_name: AI Scibe/Labs Onc 7 - Non-Squamous NSCLC - David R

golden_responses.golden_text_response: The patient has been diagnosed with non-squamous non-small cell lung cancer. Her disease is metastatic, and she has received platinum-based chemotherapy and immunotherapy already. Lab results show a ROS1 G2032R mutation, making her cancer resistant to first-generation tyrosine-kinase inhibitors. The patient wants to take as few pills as possible. Therefore, the most appropriate next treatment option is talectrinib. Talectrinib is a kinase inhibitor indicated, and U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) approved for the treatment of adult patients with locally advanced or metastatic ROS1-positive non-small cell lung cancer, and it has been shown to be effective against ROS1 G2032R mutations. Talectrinib is given orally at 600 mg daily.
